page: Google Cloud Platform x4-megamem-1440-metal is a bare-metal server instance belonging to the x4 server family. It features an x86_64 CPU architecture with 1440 dedicated virtual CPUs and 24,576 GB of system memory. This configuration provides a memory-to-core ratio of 17.07 GB per core, positioning the server in the ultra-high-capacity memory-optimized class.

The instance does not include any local storage or bundled GPUs, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached storage options. Additionally, it does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The absence of local NVMe storage and hardware accelerators represents a clear tradeoff, focusing the server's resources entirely on massive memory capacity and high-core-count CPU compute.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the bare-metal design and extreme resource density of the x4-megamem-1440-metal target workloads requiring massive in-memory databases, large-scale enterprise resource planning systems, and complex memory-bound simulations. The consolidated hardware profile reduces the overhead of managing multiple smaller instances, though the lack of local storage requires architectural planning for network storage latency.
description: Google Cloud Platform x4-megamem-1440-metal is an x86_64 bare-metal server designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads. It features 1440 dedicated vCPUs and 24,576 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 17.07 GB per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, requiring external network storage for data persistence. This hardware profile is engineered for massive in-memory databases, large-scale enterprise resource planning systems, and memory-bound data processing tasks. By consolidating compute and memory resources into a single bare-metal host, it offers high resource density for workloads that cannot be easily distributed across smaller nodes, though users must account for network storage dependencies.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform x4-megamem-1440-metal is a bare-metal server featuring 1440 dedicated vCPUs and 24,576 GB of RAM, designed for massive in-memory databases and enterprise workloads.

tagline: An ultra-high-capacity bare-metal server featuring massive memory density and dedicated compute cores for memory-bound enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 24,576 GB of RAM with 17.07 GB per core ratio
Bare-metal deployment model for direct hardware access
No local storage or integrated GPU accelerators
Designed for massive in-memory databases and ERP systems
